response.justification: Strong geospatial background via GISCollective and solid distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, but the candidate's modern C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and they have no Rust experience. Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which is adjacent to C++ but not a substitute for the role's core requirement. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to ramp-up on C++.
strengths: Nearly a decade running an open-source geospatial/GIS platform (GISCollective), directly relevant to routing/map data domain
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Linux server application experience and demonstrated ability to pick up new languages across stacks
concerns: No Rust; C++ experience is brief (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015) and outdated — falls short of 'modern C++ in commercial environments'
No explicit routing/tiling or navigation guidance pipeline experience; GIS work is web/data-collection focused, not production routing
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node-heavy product work, indicating drift away from systems-level engineering required here
